entities.name: PIGEONPEA
entities.description: A legume crop characterized as an orphan crop with limited genetic diversity and a long crop cycle

entities.name: GENETIC DIVERSITY
entities.description: The range of genetic characteristics within the cultivated gene pool of a crop

entities.name: CROP CYCLE
entities.description: The time taken from sowing to maturity of the crop

entities.name: PUBLIC FUNDING SUPPORT
entities.description: Financial resources provided by the government or public institutions for agricultural research

entities.name: ORPHAN CROP
entities.description: A crop that is under-researched and under-funded compared to major food crops

entities.name: GENETIC STOCKS
entities.description: Collections of genetic material used for crop improvement

entities.name: GENOMICS RESOURCES
entities.description: Tools and data derived from the study of the genome

entities.name: GENOME SEQUENCE
entities.description: The process of determining the complete DNA sequence of an organism's genome

entities.name: GENETIC MAPS
entities.description: The representation of the relative positions of genes or markers on a chromosome

entities.name: MARKERS
entities.description: DNA sequences used to identify specific locations on a chromosome for trait association

entities.name: MARKER-TRAITS ASSOCIATIONS
entities.description: The statistical link between a genetic marker and a specific phenotypic trait

entities.name: BREEDING PROGRAMS
entities.description: Systematic efforts to develop superior crop varieties or hybrids

entities.name: GENETIC GAINS
entities.description: The increase in the average performance of a crop population over time through breeding

entities.name: VARIETIES
entities.description: Distinct groups of plants within a species with specific desirable traits

entities.name: HYBRIDS
entities.description: Offspring resulting from the cross-breeding of two different varieties

entities.name: FOOD SUSTAINABILITY
entities.description: The ability to maintain food production and security over the long term

entities.name: NUTRITIONAL SUSTAINABILITY
entities.description: The ability to maintain the nutritional quality of food sources over time

entities.name: ENVIRONMENTAL SUSTAINABILITY
entities.description: The ability to maintain ecological balance while producing crops

entities.name: DEVELOPING COUNTRIES
entities.description: Regions characterized by lower industrialization and economic development
